surface.name: GoCardless Getting Started through the first test payment authorisation
surface.selection_basis: Under the workshop policy, the direct HTTP/cURL tab in the numbered Getting Started sequence is the fastest documented route because it avoids a language SDK installation and leads to the explicit first test payment-authorisation milestone. Sandbox signup is one opaque step at the official URL.

alternatives_considered.name: Create a payment
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is the next Getting Started stage after the first payment-authorisation request.

alternatives_considered.name: Direct Debit mandate guide
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a deeper product flow rather than the general first-request route.

entry_point.developer_intent: Follow the official GoCardless getting-started route from no account to the first test payment authorisation.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Create your first test payment authorisation in 5 minutes
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The developer creates and completes a sandbox Billing Request, then sees the resulting customer in the GoCardless dashboard.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The newly created customer appears on the Customers page after the hosted sandbox Billing Request is completed.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Create your first test payment authorisation in 5 minutes.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The guide ends its first payment-authorisation walkthrough by verifying the created customer. Creating an actual test payment is the next stage.

prerequisites.requirement: Create a free GoCardless Sandbox account; the public official sources do not enumerate the signup sequence.
Create and securely copy a Read-Write sandbox access token from the Developers tab.
Use a developer environment capable of making GoCardless API requests.

primary_path.action: Open the official Set up page and choose the Sandbox environment used by the guide.
details: The Sandbox dashboard is `manage-sandbox.gocardless.com` and the Sandbox API is `https://api-sandbox.gocardless.com/`.
primary_path.output: The sandbox account and token prerequisites
primary_path.success_signal: The Set up step is visible.

primary_path.action: Sign up for or sign in to the GoCardless Sandbox.
details: Treat signup as one opaque workshop-policy step at the official URL.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Follow the displayed account recovery path.

primary_path.action: Create a Read-Write sandbox access token.
details: Open Developers, select Create access token, provide a token name, choose Read-Write, and copy the token when displayed.
primary_path.success_signal: The one-time token value is copied.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Create a replacement token if the value was not saved.

primary_path.action: Create a BACS Billing Request with cURL.
details: Export the token and POST a `mandate_request` with scheme `bacs` to the sandbox billing_requests endpoint.
Record the returned Billing Request ID.
primary_path.success_signal: The response contains a Billing Request ID such as `BRQ123`.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Correct authorization, headers, endpoint, or request JSON.

primary_path.action: Create a Billing Request Flow for the Billing Request.
details: POST the Billing Request ID to the sandbox billing_request_flows endpoint.
primary_path.success_signal: The response contains a hosted authorisation URL.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Verify the Billing Request ID and request payload.

primary_path.action: Open the hosted authorisation URL and complete the sandbox payer flow.
details: Use the test bank details published by the guide.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Correct test payer details and retry in Sandbox.

primary_path.action: Open Customers in the sandbox dashboard and verify the new customer.
primary_path.success_signal: The newly created customer is visible on the Customers page.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Refresh after the hosted flow completes and inspect the Billing Request status.

branches.condition: After the hidden account gate, choose an official client library
branches.path: Install one of the listed official language libraries and initialize its sandbox client with the access token; the guide marks a client library as Recommended.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces direct HTTP setup while preserving the later Billing Request terminal.

branches.condition: After the hidden account gate, choose direct HTTP
branches.path: Export the access token and send authenticated HTTP or cURL requests directly.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Requires constructing request headers instead of using a client library.

friction_gates.description: The mandatory sandbox signup sequence is not publicly enumerated.
The downstream Read-Write access token is displayed once and cannot be viewed again.
The downstream setup offers a recommended client-library route and a direct-HTTP route, with language selection inside the library route.

time_to_first_success.value: 5 minutes for the first test payment authorisation

sources.title: GoCardless Sandbox sign up
evidence_supported: Absence of publicly rendered signup states

sources.title: Send your first API request
sections_used: Create your first test payment authorisation in 5 minutes

excluded_after_success: Creating the later first test payment
Live go-live checklist and production processing
